title: Les Chat Aws
stack: AWS, EC2, VPC, RDS, CloudWatch, SNS, Elastic Load Balancing, GraphQL
details: Designed a scalable, multi-tier AWS infrastructure using CloudFormation to ensure high availability and resilience.
Implemented auto-scaling and self-healing cloud infrastructure, optimizing resource utilization for better cost efficiency.
Developed a secure RDS PostgreSQL database with multi-AZ deployment, ensuring fault tolerance and data reliability.
Deployed containerized applications using Docker to enable scalable and efficient cloud-based software deployments.
Configured comprehensive monitoring with AWS CloudWatch and set real-time alerts for infrastructure monitoring.
Ensured high availability with a fault-tolerant network architecture using VPC, private subnets, and NAT Gateway.

title: DalVacationHome
stack: ReactJS, NodeJS, Python, AWS, GCP, Docker, DynamoDB
details: Architected and deployed a multi-cloud booking app on AWS and Google Cloud, ensuring scalability and uptime.
Designed an Amazon Lex Chatbot and AWS Lambda based booking logic for user interaction and support.
Automated via GitLab CI/CD, implemented Infrastructure as Code using CloudFormation, and managed Jira board.
Implemented ETL Process using BigQuery and Google Natural Language API for sentiment analysis.
Created booking and notifications using AWS SNS, SQS, and GCP Pub/Sub for real-time communication.

title: FurrEverHome
stack: SpringBoot, ReactJS, MySQL, Maven, Junit, Docker
details: Created a CI/CD pipeline with Docker and GitLab, reducing build and deployment times by 60% through caching.
Applied Test Driven Development (TDD) using JUnit, achieving 80% code coverage and reducing bugs by 50%.
Followed SDLC planning, design, development, unit testing, integration testing, and deployment to meet requirements.
Followed Agile Methodologies by completing user stories, scrum meetings, and on-time delivery, increasing client satisfaction.

title: SOFABoot – Open Source Contribution (Merged PR)
details: Authored unit tests for core components: startup/StartupReporter, compatibility/VerificationResultTests, and util/SofaBootEnvUtilsTests to harden startup metrics, compatibility checks, and environment utilities.
Coverage: Documented “Coverage Before” and “Coverage After” in the PR (via images) showing a clear increase in both line and branch coverage for the touched modules.
Expanded edge-case validation for startup reporting, verification outcomes (success/failure states), and env variable resolution to ensure deterministic behavior.
Adopted JUnit 5 + Mockito patterns for isolated, fast tests with concise assertions and clear failure messages.
PR merged into the upstream SOFABoot codebase as an open-source contribution.

title: BidSphere
stack: MongoDB, AWS, ReactJS, NodeJS, ExpressJS, SpringBoot, Microservices, Stripe
details: Architected a microservices backend with NodeJS and Spring Boot, enhancing performance and team collaboration.
Developed a document and tender management module using Spring Boot and AWS S3 for storing 5 document types.
Designed a landing page with Framer Motion’s AnimatePresence, optimizing DOM manipulation for faster load times.
Integrated Stripe’s payment gateway and APIs into Node.js microservices for secure transaction processing.

title: K8s Microservice
stack: GCP, Terraform, NodeJS, ExpressJS, Kubernetes, Microservices
details: Developed a CI/CD pipeline using Google Cloud Build, automating deployments to Google Kubernetes Engine (GKE).
Configured persistent volume for shared data storage and designed RESTful APIs for inter-service communication.
Automated GKE cluster provisioning using Terraform, optimizing infrastructure setup and deployment.

title: InkSync AWS (IaC)
stack: AWS CloudFormation, VPC, ALB, EC2 Auto Scaling, Docker, CloudWatch, SNS
details: Provisioned a production-style VPC with public/private subnets across two AZs, Internet/NAT Gateways, bastion access, and locked-down private compute.
Deployed separate frontend and backend Auto Scaling Groups via Launch Templates that install Docker and run versioned images.
Configured an Application Load Balancer with two listeners (80→frontend, 81→backend), sticky sessions, and 1-hour idle timeout for WebSockets.
Enabled CPU-based step scaling with CloudWatch alarms and email notifications via SNS for proactive ops.
Health-checked target groups with automatic unhealthy instance replacement and zero-downtime scaling.

title: Spider Bug Tracker (MERN)
stack: React, Redux Toolkit, Material-UI, React Router, Node.js, Express, MongoDB, JWT, Bcrypt, React Hook Form, date-fns
details: Built a full-stack bug tracking app with a React front end (Redux Toolkit, React Router, React Hook Form, Material-UI) and a Node.js/Express API backed by MongoDB.
Implements role-based auth with JWT; supports separate logins for admins/project leads and developers, with password hashing via bcrypt.
Project lead workflow: create, edit, and delete projects; add developers to a project; assign bugs to specific developers.
Per-bug discussion thread so teams can collaborate within the bug detail view; developers can see all bugs for their projects and a count of those assigned to them.
Repo organized as client/ and server/ workspaces with npm scripts for local dev (“npm run client”, “npm run server”); environment driven configuration.
Clean, user-friendly UI with reusable components, date formatting via date-fns, and MIT-licensed source.

title: Mini Store (MERN)
stack: React, React Router, Node.js, Express, MongoDB, REST API, Netlify
details: Built a MERN e-commerce web app for neighborhood general stores so customers can browse products by category, view trending items, and shop from nearby shops.
Implements authentication flows (Sign Up/Sign In) with an Account area, a persistent Cart, and a checkout UI with clear CTAs and delivery/payment option messaging.
Organized as client (SPA) and server (API) workspaces; the server exposes REST endpoints for products, shops, users, and cart operations while the client consumes them via Axios/fetch.
Responsive single-page routing for Home, Account, Cart, Shops, and Products, plus promotional sections (e.g., seasonal sale banners, delivery flexibility, multiple payment options).
Deployed the frontend to Netlify for quick previews and sharing; repository is a customized fork adapted to this project’s requirements.
